steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank follows "despite the education specialist's ______ her expertise on the subject in general." The word "despite" signals a contrast: her state regarding her expertise must conflict with her ability to speak authoritatively about children's literature. Look for a word meaning uncertainty or reluctance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "affinity for": "Affinity for" means a liking or attraction. If she had an affinity for her expertise, that would align with speaking authoritatively — no contrast is created. ✗
  • "exceptions to": "Exceptions to her expertise" is semantically awkward and does not produce a meaningful contrast with speaking authoritatively. ✗
  • "hesitancy about": "Hesitancy about her expertise" means she doubted or was unsure of her general knowledge. "Despite" that hesitancy, she still managed to speak authoritatively about children's literature — a clear and logical contrast. ✓
  • "insight into": "Insight into her expertise" implies she understood her own knowledge well, which would support speaking authoritatively rather than contrast with it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
